title: Chapter Sixty-Nine: The Thirty-Six Afflictions
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

"You must learn the doctrine," the Blood Flame Sect Leader said, pouring Du Kang wine with the delicacy of a man handling silk. "To teach the Grand Teacher, ordinary rank will not do. I should assign an elder. But the first key point -- I want to tell you myself."

Yeon-shin touched his cup without drinking.

"The essence of our doctrine is enough in one phrase." The Sect Leader leaned forward. "Our sect burns the world's chaos and distress."

"Afflictions."

A satisfied smile. "Buddhist monks call them that. These thirty-six steps have great meaning. We built them to overcome the thirty-six afflictions of the mundane world. Our teachings are no different from Maitreya's salvation."

"Is death salvation?"

The smile deepened.

"If we discuss the profound meaning, it would not be enough debating through many dawns. Do you know the Ming dynasty was wrong from birth? The world has already fallen. You must know of the poor harvests spreading across the Central Plains."

He lightly kicked the step beneath him. There was a boyish quality to the gesture -- unbefitting an absolute power of the martial world.

"Bad years bring chaotic times. Since the murim is not a world of immortals but of those who must eat, what follows is clear. Famine. Plunder. The boundary between government and martial world will collapse."

"Desolate Sect will be busy."

"Your sect is powerful. But small, compared to the vast Central Plains. The day will come when the power of martial artists becomes omnipotent authority. Martial arts above the law. The Hwangbo family's actions already proved it."

Yeon-shin said nothing.

"When founding emperor Zhu Yuanzhang and those elf creatures raised the country, I saw clearly. The horrors of the wars between Yuan and Ming -- even our blood techniques could not match them."

"They say you have lived as long as the Nine Sects' elf elders."

"I guarantee. Calamities no less than then will come. And in that era, there will be no heroes. Raising the banner of chivalry while stomachs shrink?" He drained his cup. "Ridiculous."

The Leader's long fingers traced the rim, drawing invisible maps.

"The coexistence of martial arts and chivalry that dilettantes cry for does not exist. Only martial arts alone will remain. Our sect must spread widely. We will reduce the world's mouths with blood techniques, and sect members strengthened by sacred victims will re-establish order."

"You said you burn chaos and distress."

"To remove afflictions, one must be afflicted. It is our mission to trample the ignorant masses' lack of understanding and advance."

The exhale released with his words was solid. Almost physical. For one dangerous moment, Yeon-shin nearly took it for deep insight.

He could not.

The man contained both an absolute power's dizzying magnetism and a demon lord's madness in one body. Not wisdom. Chaos.

*Time is up.*

The sunset was fading. He gauged the hour. The day the Sect Lord had given him was gone. She could appear at any moment.

"Almighty Sect Leader!"

Gao Zong-xue's voice, shrill with resentment, rang from below. He dragged a boy by the scruff -- a commoner, barely conscious, face so swollen and beaten that his features were unrecognizable.

"The Grand Teacher has proven his talent! But he has yet to display the wonder of star absorption! I wish to present tribute -- I dare request permission!"

*Star absorption. Their holy name for draining a human being dry.*

The Blood Flame Sect Leader turned to Yeon-shin. Interest on his lips. "This seat knows it to be so. Go down. Add that boy's meager strength to your nourishment. Step into a new world."

The voice tinged with laughter instigated murder.

Yeon-shin stood. Began descending the thirty-six steps.

*Woong.*

He raised inner power. Wrapped the Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm inside the Demon Roaring Blood Technique's shell. This was a den of demons -- supreme masters everywhere, any one of them capable of seeing through ordinary martial arts in an instant.

But not this.

Blood technique and dharma power. Opposing energies harmonized with innate sense. The fusion was powerful -- more powerful than either alone.

He reached Gao Zong-xue. Looked past him to the boy sprawled on the ground.

Spirit in those swollen eyes. Defiance.

"Detestable... blood demons... Father will not forgive..."

The boy glared at Yeon-shin with all the strength left in his broken body.

Yeon-shin stretched out his hand. Irregular blood technique waves spread from each finger joint.

The technique aimed not at the boy.

At Gao Zong-xue's neck.

The man smirked. Raised protective energy -- a solid red haze, thick with blood technique cultivation. He did not dodge. Why would he? This was a child's tantrum. A worm lashing out.

"The noble ones can fold their convictions before the greater cause. Whether the Grand Teacher is noble is another--"

*Crunch.*

The Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm punched through his protective energy like a fist through wet paper. Yeon-shin's hand closed around Gao Zong-xue's throat. Both carotid arteries, tempered with decades of true qi cultivation, and the vertebrae beneath them -- all crumpled together in his grip.

He pushed. The corpse fell with a thud, discarded like a broken stick.

The boy's eyes went round.

"What deplorable--!"

"You bastard!"

Blood Flame Sect masters rushed in from all sides. Killing intent erupted like a physical wall.

Yeon-shin stood calm, the boy behind him. He did not move.

*Let them come.*

His upper dantian energy sense -- awakened, burning at his crown -- painted the world beyond the formation barrier. Hundreds of martial artists in ambush, surrounding the compound. Their momentum was tremendous.

And above it all, directly over the center of the sorcery formation: Desolate Sect's lord, treading on wind. Pale green robes fluttering against the sky. The bearing of a complete absolute master.

Her foot descended.

*BOOM!*

The atmosphere rippled.

*Let us return.*

He thought of Won-chang. Of Ma Jin. Of blue martial clothes and the weight of two swords at his hip.

It was time to go home.

---
